annotations.label: Definition of diffeomorphism (intro)

anchor.snippet: is a diffeomorphism if it is a bijection and its inverse

annotations.note: The structure `Diffeomorph` (notation `≃ₘ^n`) bundles a bijection (via `Equiv`) with smoothness of both the map and its inverse, directly formalizing the definition of diffeomorphism.

annotations.label: Definition of C^r-diffeomorphism
annotations.note: The `Diffeomorph` structure is parametrized by a smoothness level `n : ℕ∞ω`, so `M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I'⟯ M'` is exactly a C^n-diffeomorphism.

annotations.label: Definition of diffeomorphic manifolds
annotations.note: Two manifolds are diffeomorphic iff there exists a term of type `Diffeomorph I J M N n`. The notation `≃ₘ⟮I, J⟯` is used in Mathlib.

annotations.label: C^0-diffeomorphism is a homeomorphism
annotations.note: `Diffeomorph.toHomeomorph` converts any C^n-diffeomorphism to a homeomorphism; setting n=0 gives the C^0 case. The coercion works for all smoothness levels.

annotations.label: Smooth map between subsets of manifolds

anchor.snippet: is said to be smooth if for all

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `ContMDiffOn` for smooth maps restricted to subsets of manifolds, but the Wikipedia article's notion of smoothness on arbitrary subsets (via local extensions) is not directly formalized as a standalone definition.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism of subsets of manifolds

anchor.snippet: is said to be a diffeomorphism if it is bijective, smooth

annotations.note: `PartialDiffeomorph` formalizes diffeomorphisms between open subsets of manifolds, which is related but not identical to the Wikipedia notion of diffeomorphisms between arbitrary subsets.

annotations.label: Hadamard-Caccioppoli theorem
annotations.note: The Hadamard-Caccioppoli global inverse function theorem (a local diffeomorphism from R^n to R^n that is proper is a diffeomorphism) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex squaring map is not a diffeomorphism (simply connected needed)
annotations.note: This specific counterexample (z ↦ z² on C\{0} is a local diffeomorphism but not a diffeomorphism) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential is invertible iff it is a bijection (Jacobian criterion)

anchor.snippet: well-defined inverse if and only if

annotations.note: The inverse function theorem is formalized: if the derivative is a linear equivalence at a point, the function is locally invertible. However, the specific 'iff' statement involving the Jacobian determinant is not stated in that form.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphisms preserve dimension

anchor.snippet: Diffeomorphisms are necessarily between manifolds of the same dimension

annotations.note: Mathlib shows the differential of a diffeomorphism is a continuous linear equivalence between tangent spaces (implying equal dimension), but the explicit statement that diffeomorphic manifolds have the same dimension is not stated as a standalone theorem.

annotations.label: Definition of local diffeomorphism

anchor.snippet: is said to be a local diffeomorphism

annotations.note: `IsLocalDiffeomorph I J n f` states that every point has a neighborhood on which `f` restricts to a diffeomorphism, exactly matching the Wikipedia definition.

annotations.label: Definition of submersion

anchor.snippet: is said to be a submersion

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have a definition of submersion (surjective differential) for maps between manifolds. The concept exists in `Immersion.lean` docstrings as future work but has not been implemented.

annotations.label: Definition of immersion

anchor.snippet: is said to be an immersion

annotations.note: `IsImmersion I J n f` formalizes the notion of an immersion (injective differential at every point) between smooth manifolds.

annotations.label: x^3 is a homeomorphism but not a diffeomorphism

anchor.snippet: differentiable bijection is not necessarily a diffeomorphism

annotations.note: This specific counterexample (x ↦ x³ on R is a differentiable bijection whose inverse is not differentiable at 0) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every diffeomorphism is a homeomorphism (but not conversely)
annotations.note: `Diffeomorph.toHomeomorph` provides the coercion from any diffeomorphism to a homeomorphism, directly formalizing this statement.

annotations.label: Explicit map f with Jacobian zero on axes

anchor.snippet: zero determinant if and only if

annotations.note: This specific computational example about a map R² → R² with Jacobian vanishing on the coordinate axes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local diffeomorphism criterion via linear independence of linear terms

anchor.snippet: local diffeomorphism at 0 if, and only if

annotations.note: This criterion (checking linear independence of the linear parts of component functions) is a restatement of the inverse function theorem for specific coordinate expressions, not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Map h with everywhere-zero Jacobian determinant (image is the unit circle)
annotations.note: This specific counterexample (a smooth map R² → R² with vanishing Jacobian everywhere whose image is the unit circle) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobian matrix of a surface deformation

anchor.snippet: stress-induced transformation is called a deformation

annotations.note: The notion of surface deformation as used in applied mathematics / continuum mechanics is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Surface diffeomorphisms preserve angles (conformal property)
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `ConformalAt` and `Conformal` for maps between normed spaces, but the specific claim that certain surface diffeomorphisms are conformal is not stated.

annotations.label: Definition of the diffeomorphism group
annotations.note: Mathlib defines composition (`trans`), identity (`refl`), and inverse (`symm`) for diffeomorphisms, giving the group operations, but `Diffeomorph I I M M n` is not bundled as a `Group` instance.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group is not locally compact (for positive-dimensional M)
annotations.note: This topological property of the diffeomorphism group (not locally compact) is not formalized; Mathlib does not study the topology of function spaces of diffeomorphisms.

annotations.label: Weak and strong topologies agree on compact manifolds

anchor.snippet: two natural topologies: weak and strong

annotations.note: The weak (compact-open) and strong (Whitney) topologies on spaces of smooth maps, and their agreement for compact manifolds, are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak topology is always metrizable
annotations.note: Metrizability of the compact-open topology on smooth map spaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong topology is Baire but not metrizable (noncompact case)
annotations.note: Properties of the strong (Whitney) topology on smooth map spaces are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group is locally homeomorphic to space of vector fields

anchor.snippet: locally homeomorphic to the space of

annotations.note: The local chart structure of the diffeomorphism group (via the exponential map to vector fields) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group is a Banach manifold (finite r, compact M)

anchor.snippet: Banach manifold with smooth right translations

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Banach manifolds or the infinite-dimensional manifold structure on diffeomorphism groups.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group is a Frechet Lie group (r = infinity, compact M)
annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Frechet manifolds or Frechet Lie groups. The `LieGroup` class exists but is for finite-dimensional smooth manifolds.

annotations.label: Lie algebra of the diffeomorphism group

anchor.snippet: Lie algebra of the diffeomorphism group

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Lie bracket of vector fields on manifolds (`mlieBracket`), which is the Lie algebra operation of Diff(M), but does not identify it as the Lie algebra of the diffeomorphism group.

annotations.label: Natural inclusion of a Lie group in its diffeomorphism group
annotations.note: The embedding of a Lie group G into Diff(G) via left multiplication is not formalized as such in Mathlib, though the smoothness of left multiplication is established for Lie groups.

annotations.label: Diff(R^n) has two components; GL is a deformation retract

anchor.snippet: general linear group is a deformation retract

annotations.note: The homotopy type of Diff(R^n) and the deformation retraction to GL(n,R) are deep results in differential topology not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group of a finite set is the symmetric group

anchor.snippet: diffeomorphism group is simply the symmetric group

annotations.note: This identification requires viewing finite sets as 0-dimensional manifolds, which is outside Mathlib's current manifold framework. The symmetric group itself is `Equiv.Perm` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group acts transitively on connected manifold
annotations.note: Transitivity of the diffeomorphism group action on a connected manifold is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Action on configuration space is multiply transitive (dim >= 2)
annotations.note: Multiple transitivity of the diffeomorphism group action on configuration spaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic extension of circle diffeomorphism yields disc diffeomorphism (Rado-Kneser-Choquet)
annotations.note: The Rado-Kneser-Choquet theorem on harmonic extensions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orientation-preserving diffeomorphism group of the circle is pathwise connected

anchor.snippet: diffeomorphism group of the circle is pathwise connected

annotations.note: Path-connectedness of Diff+(S^1) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diff(S^1) has the homotopy type of O(2)
annotations.note: The homotopy type of Diff(S^1) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group of twisted spheres
annotations.note: The group of twisted spheres (Γ_n) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mapping class group
annotations.note: The mapping class group (π₀ of the diffeomorphism group) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Surface mapping class group is finitely presented, generated by Dehn twists

anchor.snippet: finitely presented group generated by Dehn twists

annotations.note: Dehn twists and finite presentation of the mapping class group are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mapping class group equals outer automorphism group of fundamental group (surfaces)

anchor.snippet: outer automorphism group of the fundamental group

annotations.note: The Dehn-Nielsen-Baer theorem is not formalized. Mathlib has `FundamentalGroup` but not outer automorphism groups or this isomorphism.

annotations.label: Thurston classification: periodic, reducible, or pseudo-Anosov
annotations.note: The Nielsen-Thurston classification of surface diffeomorphisms is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Identity component of orientation-preserving diffeomorphism group is simple (Thurston)
annotations.note: Thurston's simplicity theorem for the identity component of Diff(M) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diff(S^2) has the homotopy type of O(3) (Smale)
annotations.note: Smale's theorem on the homotopy type of Diff(S^2) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diff(T^2) has the homotopy type of GL(2,Z) x T^2
annotations.note: The homotopy type of Diff(T^2) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diff(surface of genus >= 2) has contractible components
annotations.note: Earle-Eells theorem on contractibility of components of Diff for higher-genus surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diff(S^n) does not have the homotopy type of a finite CW-complex (n >= 6)

anchor.snippet: does not have the homotopy-type of a finite CW-complex

annotations.note: This result about the homotopy type of Diff(S^n) for n >= 6 is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphic implies homeomorphic (converse fails in general)
annotations.note: `Diffeomorph.toHomeomorph` gives the forward implication. The converse failing is witnessed by `exists_homeomorph_isEmpty_diffeomorph_sphere_seven` (a proof_wanted in PoincareConjecture.lean).

annotations.label: In dimensions 1, 2, 3 homeomorphic smooth manifolds are diffeomorphic

anchor.snippet: homeomorphic but not diffeomorphic pairs exist

annotations.note: The theorem that homeomorphic smooth manifolds in dimensions <= 3 are diffeomorphic (Moise for dim 3) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Milnor's exotic 7-sphere
annotations.note: The existence of an exotic 7-sphere is stated as a `proof_wanted` in Mathlib: a manifold homeomorphic to S^7 but with no diffeomorphism to S^7. The proof is not yet provided.

annotations.label: 28 oriented diffeomorphism classes of manifolds homeomorphic to S^7
annotations.note: The classification of exotic 7-spheres (28 oriented diffeomorphism classes) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exotic R^4: uncountably many non-diffeomorphic smooth structures
annotations.note: Mathlib states the existence of a small exotic R^4 as a `proof_wanted`, but not the uncountability of exotic smooth structures. The full result is far from formalized.
